---
name: Bug Report
about: Submit a formal bug report to help improve PulseStack's reliability and observability
title: "[Bug]: <Brief description of the issue>"
labels: bug, triage
assignees: ''
---

## Bug Description
<!-- Please provide a clear, comprehensive, and formal description of the issue you have encountered. Explain what happened, how it deviated from the expected behavior, and the impact it has on the system. -->

## Prerequisites
<!-- Before submitting, please check the following: -->
- [ ] I have verified that this issue has not already been reported.
- [ ] I have checked the documentation and believe this is a genuine malfunction, not a configuration error.
- [ ] I am using the latest stable release or the `main` branch of PulseStack.

## Steps to Reproduce
<!-- Please provide explicit, reproducible steps. The more detail you provide, the faster our engineers can diagnose the problem. -->
1. Initialize the system in the following state: `...`
2. Execute the following command or API request: `...`
3. Observe the output or state change...

## Expected Behavior
<!-- Formally describe the anticipated outcome. What should the system have done under normal operating conditions? -->

## Actual Behavior
<!-- Describe the erroneous outcome. What did the system actually do? -->

## Diagnostic Information
<!-- To assist in debugging, please provide relevant environment and runtime details. -->

### Environment Details
- **Operating System:** [e.g., Ubuntu 22.04 LTS, macOS 14.1]
- **Node.js Version:** [e.g., v20.12.0]
- **Kubernetes / Docker Version:** [if applicable]
- **PulseStack Version / Commit Hash:** [e.g., v0.1.0 or abc1234]

### System Logs & Stack Traces
<!-- Paste any relevant system logs, crash reports, or stack traces below. Please enclose them in code blocks to ensure readability. -->
```text
<Insert logs here>
```

### Trace IDs & Observability Data
<!-- If this relates to distributed tracing or telemetry, please provide relevant Trace IDs or metric anomalies. -->

## Additional Context
<!-- Provide any other pertinent information, such as recent infrastructure changes, network conditions, or architectural context that may have contributed to this issue. -->
